name: Pelthos Therapeutics Inc.
business: Pelthos Therapeutics Inc. is a bio-pharmaceutical company with three FDA-approved products: ZELSUVMI for molluscum contagiosum, XEPI for impetigo, and XEGLYZE for head lice. The Company sells ZELSUVMI primarily to national and regional wholesaler channels, which supply retail drug store chains, independent pharmacies, and mail order pharmacies.
namedCustomers: Three wholesalers accounted for 89% of gross revenue at 35%, 28% and 26%, respectively

exposures.sentence: The Company currently has three U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approved products in its commercial portfolio, in various stages of commercialization, including ZELSUVMI™, XEPI, and XEGLYZE.

catalysts.sentence: The Company expects to commercially relaunch XEPI once certain manufacturing, supply chain, and regulatory activities are validated, implemented and completed, respectively. In addition, the Company will also need to implement its commercial marketing, trade and access strategy prior to the relaunch of XEPI, currently expected in late 2026.

The Company expects to commercially launch XEGLYZE once certain manufacturing, supply chain, and regulatory activities are validated, implemented and completed, respectively. In addition, the Company will also need to implement its commercial marketing, trade and access strategy prior to the launch of XEGLYZE, currently expected in 2027.

catalysts.event: Initiation of Phase 1a/2b study in Eye Pain
catalysts.sentence: The Company expects to initiate the Phase 1a/2b study in Eye Pain in the first quarter of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Molluscum contagiosum affects approximately 16 million people of all ages in the United States.
tamClaims.sentence: The Company estimates that molluscum contagiosum infections afflict approximately 16 million people of all ages in the United States.

tamClaims.claim: Impetigo affects approximately 3 million people in the U.S. every year.
tamClaims.sentence: Impetigo affects approximately 3 million people in the U.S. every year and is among the most common bacterial skin infections seen in pediatric offices.

tamClaims.claim: Head lice infestations: an estimated 6 to 12 million infestations occur each year in the U.S. among children 3 to 11 years of age.
tamClaims.sentence: An estimated 6 to 12 million infestations occur each year in the U.S. among children 3 to 11 years of age.
